assertions.title: All Scalar Token Types Lexed Correctly

steps.action: Run 17 tests: TestLexPlainValue, TestLexSimpleKeyStringValues, TestLexComplexStringValues, TestLexSimpleKeyIntegerValues, TestLexSimpleKeyNegativeIntegerValues, TestLexSimpleKeyFloatValues, TestLexSimpleKeyBoolValues, TestLexAllBoolLiterals, TestLexDateValues, TestLexVariableValues, TestLexInclude, TestLexAllSizeSuffixes, TestLexConvenientIntegerValues, TestLexEmptyStringDQ, TestLexEmptyStringSQ, TestLexItemString, TestLexRawString

Verify test source covers all scalar categories: strings (DQ, SQ, raw, empty), integers (+/-), size suffixes (k/K/m/M/g/G/t/T/p/P/e/E/KB/MB/GB/TB/PB/EB/Ki/Mi/Gi/Ti/Pi/Ei/KiB/MiB/GiB/TiB/PiB/EiB/Kb/Mb/Gb/Tb/Pb/Eb), floats, booleans (true/false/yes/no/on/off case-insensitive), datetime (ISO8601 Zulu), variables ($name), includes
steps.expected: Every scalar category is exercised with exact token type and value assertions matching v1
steps.observed: All categories confirmed present. TestLexAllBoolLiterals tests all 12 case variants of 6 bool literals. TestLexAllSizeSuffixes tests 36 suffix variants. TestLexEmptyStringDQ/SQ test empty strings. TestLexRawString tests unquoted strings. TestLexSimpleKeyStringValues tests DQ/SQ. TestLexComplexStringValues tests SQ with escape chars preserved literally.

steps.action: Run 6 tests: TestLexColonKeySep, TestLexWhitespaceKeySep, TestLexQuotedKeys, TestLexQuotedKeysWithSpace, TestLexMapQuotedKeys, TestLexSpecialCharsMapQuotedKeys
Verify test source covers: equals (foo = 123), colon (foo : 123, foo:123, foo: 123), whitespace (foo 123, foo\t123), single/double quoted keys with spaces and special characters
steps.expected: All separator styles and quoted key variants exercised
steps.observed: TestLexColonKeySep covers colon with various spacing including 'foo:123', 'foo: 123', 'foo : 123', 'foo:  123\r\n'. TestLexWhitespaceKeySep covers space and tab separators. TestLexQuotedKeys covers both 'foo' and "foo" quoted keys. TestLexQuotedKeysWithSpace covers keys with embedded spaces. TestLexMapQuotedKeys covers quoted keys inside map blocks. TestLexSpecialCharsMapQuotedKeys covers keys like 'bar-1.2.3' with special characters in nested maps.

steps.action: Run 13 tests: TestLexEscapedString, TestLexBinaryString, TestLexBinaryStringLatin1, TestLexSingleQuotedNoEscape, TestLexCompoundStringEE, TestLexCompoundStringEES, TestLexCompoundStringES, TestLexCompoundStringESE, TestLexCompoundStringSE, TestLexCompoundStringSEE, TestLexCompoundStringSES, TestLexNonBool, TestLexNonVariable
Verify test source covers: \t, \n, \r, \", \\, \xHH in both quoted and unquoted contexts; single-quoted raw passthrough; escaped backslash prefix suppresses bool/variable detection
steps.expected: All escape sequence types verified with correct final values
steps.observed: TestLexEscapedString verifies \t->tab, \r->CR, \n->NL, \"->quote, \\->backslash in unquoted context. TestLexBinaryString/Latin1 verifies \x65->"e" and \xe9->0xe9 byte. TestLexCompoundString* (7 tests) verify mixed literal+escaped strings in all orderings (ES, SE, EE, SEE, SES, EES, ESE). TestLexSingleQuotedNoEscape confirms 'bar\r\n  \t' preserves backslashes literally. TestLexNonBool confirms \\true -> "\true" (string, not bool). TestLexNonVariable confirms \\$var -> "\$var" (string, not variable).

steps.action: Verify error tokens for: invalid escape char (\y), incomplete \x at EOL (0 and 1 hex chars), invalid hex chars after \x in quoted strings
steps.expected: Error tokens match v1 output for all invalid escape cases
steps.observed: TestLexBadStringEscape: \y produces ItemError 'Invalid escape character 'y'. Only the following escape characters are allowed: \xXX, \t, \n, \r, \", \\.' at line 1 pos 8. TestLexBadBinaryStringEndingAfterZeroHexChars: \x at EOL produces 'Expected two hexadecimal digits after '\x', but hit end of line'. TestLexBadBinaryStringEndingAfterOneHexChar: \xF at EOL produces same message. TestLexBadBinaryStringWithZeroHexChars: \x]" in quoted string produces 'Expected two hexadecimal digits after '\x', but got ']"''. TestLexBadBinaryStringWithOneHexChar: \xe] in quoted string produces 'Expected two hexadecimal digits after '\x', but got 'e]''.

steps.action: Run 3 tests: TestLexCRLFSupport, TestLexCRLFMultiline, TestLexCRLFAllValueTypes (18 subtests)
steps.expected: All tests pass, CRLF treated identically to LF
steps.observed: All 3 tests passed including 18 subtests (PASS, 0.109s). TestLexCRLFAllValueTypes covers: string_dq, string_sq, integer, integer_suffix, float, bool, datetime, variable, comment_hash, comment_slash, array, map, include, semicolons, ip_addr, raw_string, empty_string_dq, empty_string_sq

steps.action: Verify test methodology: CRLF inputs compared token-by-token against LF inputs for type and value equality
steps.expected: Token types and values match between LF and CRLF versions for all value types
steps.observed: TestLexCRLFSupport compares individual tokens from LF vs CRLF of same input. TestLexCRLFMultiline verifies multiline CRLF input produces correct key/value sequence. TestLexCRLFAllValueTypes uses strings.ReplaceAll to create CRLF versions and compares all token types and values.

steps.action: Run 5 tests: TestLexIntegerOverflow, TestLexNegativeIntegerOverflow, TestLexIntegerOverflowWithSuffix, TestLexFloatOverflow, TestLexValidLargeInteger
Verify: int64 overflow returns error, negative int64 overflow returns error, suffix overflow returns error, float overflow handled at lex level, valid max int64 passes
steps.expected: Overflow cases produce ItemError with 'integer range' message; valid large integers accepted
steps.observed: TestLexIntegerOverflow: 99999999999999999999 produces ItemError containing 'integer range'. TestLexNegativeIntegerOverflow: -99999999999999999999 produces ItemError containing 'integer range'. TestLexIntegerOverflowWithSuffix: 99999999999999999999k produces ItemError containing 'integer range'. TestLexFloatOverflow: large decimal float lexes as ItemFloat (overflow checked at parse time, not lex time). TestLexValidLargeInteger: 9223372036854775807 (max int64) lexes as ItemInteger with correct value.

steps.expected: Test passes, negative floats lexed as float tokens with negative sign

steps.action: Verify: -22.2 and -0.5 lexed as ItemFloat with negative sign included in value
steps.observed: TestLexNegativeFloatValues verifies 'foo = -22.2' produces {ItemFloat, "-22.2", 1, 6} and 'foo = -0.5' produces {ItemFloat, "-0.5", 1, 6}. Both include the negative sign in the token value.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: all 7 passed. 49 total test functions executed across all assertions covering all scalar types, key separators, escape sequences, error reporting, CRLF support, numeric overflow, and negative floats. Test source code verified to exercise all behaviors described in each assertion.
